name: haldir
description: Cryptographic governance layer for AI agents. Scoped sessions, encrypted vault, RFC 6962 Merkle-tree tamper-evident audit log with Ed25519 Signed Tree Heads, Sigstore Rekor mirror, anti-equivocation verifier, x402 pay-per-request surface, and 2-line adoption in LangChain, CrewAI, and LlamaIndex.

authentication.bootstrap: POST /v1/keys (first key per tenant is free; subsequent keys require an existing admin key)

x402.description: Agents buy individual audit proofs with USDC on Base Sepolia; three paid resources live today ($0.001–$0.10 per call).

identity_and_access: Scope check endpoint for gating any tool call
secrets: AES-256-GCM encrypted vault with AAD tenant binding
Scope-gated retrieval (agents never see raw credentials when used correctly)
audit: RFC 6962 Merkle tree over every entry
Ed25519 Signed Tree Heads; public key at /.well-known/jwks.json
External transparency mirror (Sigstore Rekor / file / HTTP archiver)
Offline-verifiable inclusion + consistency proofs via the Python SDK
compliance: Signed audit-prep evidence packs relevant to SOC2 CC5.2, CC6.1, CC6.7, CC7.2, CC7.3, CC8.1
Live readiness score (0-100) with per-criterion remediation hints
governance: Webhook alerts with HMAC-SHA256 signing + 24h secret rotation
Policy-enforcement proxy for MCP tool calls

trust_signals.external_mirror: Configurable via HALDIR_TRANSPARENCY_MIRROR; backends: file, http, rekor
trust_signals.rekor_receipt_verifier: Every stored mirror receipt is cryptographically verifiable against Rekor's own published key. Endpoint: /v1/audit/sth-log/mirror/receipts/<id>/verify
trust_signals.offline_sdk_verification: haldir.verify_inclusion_proof / verify_consistency_proof / verify_sth / verify_rekor_receipt all run offline with no trust in Haldir

listed_in: https://github.com/bh-rat/EMERGING.md (Security & Governance section, merged via PR #55)
